title: Notylia inodora
intro: Notylia inodora, also known as the wild orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Notylia inodora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its health benefits. Notylia inodora is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and wounds.
